deep_dive.title: Prob-BBDM: a Probabilistic Brownian Bridge Diffusion Model for MRI sequence image-to-image translation
deep_dive.abstract: AI-driven image-to-image synthesis is rapidly advancing, with growing applications in medical imaging. Multi-modal image analysis plays a crucial role in optimizing examination quality, yet acquiring multiple imaging modalities in clinical settings remains resource-intensive and time-consuming, especially for 3D imaging. To address this challenge, we propose a novel image-to-image translation model based on Brownian Bridge Diffusion Models (BBDM), which synthesizes magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) sequences from 2D axial slices. Our approach integrates a variational encoder-guided diffusion mechanism, leveraging probabilistic image distributions to enhance synthesis quality. Evaluated on the BraTS 2021 dataset, our Probabilistic-BBDM (Prob-BBDM) achieves superior performance across multiple translation tasks, reaching up to 88.46% SSIM and 26.09 dB PSNR, with consistent improvements over baselines. Notably, our diffusion process requires only 4 steps, making it computationally efficient while maintaining high-quality synthesis. To further validate generalizability, we test Prob-BBDM on an external third-party dataset, demonstrating consistent performance across domains. Additionally, we assess the clinical utility of the synthesized slices by using them as input to a pre-trained segmentation model. Tumor segmentation yields a Dice score of 88.71% and an HD95 of 3.49 mm, confirming that the synthesized slices preserve critical diagnostic information. These results highlight the potential of Prob-BBDM for high-quality, efficient, and generalizable MRI synthesis, offering a promising step toward improved medical image translation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: uses Brownian Bridge Diffusion Model for image-to-image translation, bridging two distributions
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用布朗桥扩散模型进行图像翻译,桥接两个分布
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用布朗桥扩散模型进行图像翻译,桥接两个分布
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用布朗桥扩散模型进行图像翻译,桥接两个分布
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Prob-BBDM, a probabilistic Brownian bridge diffusion model for MRI image-to-image translation, integrating variational encoder-guided diffusion to synthesize target sequences from source slices.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 多模态MRI获取成本高,尤其是3D成像,限制了临床应用.本文提出概率布朗桥扩散模型Prob-BBDM,通过变分编码器引导的扩散机制,从2D轴向切片合成目标MRI序列.在BraTS 2021数据集上,该方法在生成质量和模态一致性上显著优于现有方法,且计算高效.该工作展示了布朗桥扩散在医学图像翻译中的潜力,并为构建任意分布间的桥接提供了通用框架.
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 多模态MRI获取成本高,尤其是3D成像,限制了临床应用.本文提出概率布朗桥扩散模型Prob-BBDM,通过变分编码器引导的扩散机制,从2D轴向切片合成目标MRI序列.在BraTS 2021数据集上,该方法在生成质量和模态一致性上显著优于现有方法,且计算高效.该工作展示了布朗桥扩散在医学图像翻译中的潜力,并为构建任意分布间的桥接提供了通用框架.
deep_dive.matched_query_text: diffusion bridge connecting two probability distributions

quick_skim.title: Score Approximation for Diffusion Models on Arbitrary Low-Dimensional Structures
quick_skim.abstract: The remarkable success of score-based diffusion models has spurred significant efforts to establish their theoretical foundations. However, existing complexity bounds for score approximation rely heavily on restrictive assumptions like Lipschitz continuous densities or smooth manifold supports, which are routinely violated by the singularities, sharp boundaries, and disjoint clusters inherent to real-world perceptual data. This work establishes a universal score approximation theorem that works for any distribution supported on any compact set of upper Minkowski dimension $d$. Using a novel discrete-mixture formulation, we prove that the score function can be approximated with a ReLU network whose complexity grows exponentially only with $d$, thus breaking the exponential curse of ambient dimensionality. Combined with existing theories on accurately solving the backward diffusion SDE for arbitrary compact distributions, our work shows that diffusion models readily adapt to irregular, non-smooth data structures, explaining their competence in real-world generative tasks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: score approximation for diffusion models enables score-based Schrodinger bridge solvers
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 扩散模型的分数近似为基于分数的薛定谔桥求解器提供支撑
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 扩散模型的分数近似为基于分数的薛定谔桥求解器提供支撑
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 扩散模型的分数近似为基于分数的薛定谔桥求解器提供支撑
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Establishes universal score approximation theorem for diffusion models on low-dimensional structures, providing theoretical foundation for score-based methods used in Schrodinger bridge solving.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文建立了扩散模型在任意低维结构上的通用分数近似定理。通过新颖的离散混合表述，证明了只要分布支撑在Minkowski维数为d的紧集上，分数函数可用ReLU网络逼近，复杂度仅随d指数增长。该理论去除了对Lipschitz连续密度或光滑流形支撑的限制，为基于分数的扩散模型（包括薛定谔桥求解）提供了坚实的理论基础。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文建立了扩散模型在任意低维结构上的通用分数近似定理。通过新颖的离散混合表述，证明了只要分布支撑在Minkowski维数为d的紧集上，分数函数可用ReLU网络逼近，复杂度仅随d指数增长。该理论去除了对Lipschitz连续密度或光滑流形支撑的限制，为基于分数的扩散模型（包括薛定谔桥求解）提供了坚实的理论基础。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Solving the Schrödinger bridge problem using score based diffusion models

quick_skim.title: The Geometry Behind Diffusion and Flow Matching: Gradient Flows and Geodesics in Wasserstein Space
quick_skim.abstract: The space $\mathcal{P}_2(\mathbb{R}^d$) of probability measures with finite second moment carries a natural geometry: the quadratic Wasserstein distance W_2 makes it a complete metric space and, following Otto, a (formal) Riemannian manifold whose geodesics are the optimal-transport interpolations. On this manifold, the gradient flow of the free energy F(rho) = KL(rho || π) is exactly the Fokker-Planck equation, and its implicit-Euler discretization is the JKO scheme. This is the geometry underlying diffusion models: the forward process descends the free energy, and each denoising step realizes one JKO step, which recovers DDPM, DDIM, NCSN/SMLD, and Energy Matching; this is one scheme, not separate theories. The same manifold supports a second variational principle. Its geodesics - the minimum-action curves of the Benamou-Brenier formula - are precisely the optimal-transport paths that Flow Matching learns. Fixing both endpoints and following the geodesic, generation becomes a deterministic ODE along a straight line, hence far fewer sampling steps. Placing both families of models on one manifold makes their relationship exact: diffusion follows a free-energy gradient flow, an initial-value problem; optimal-transport Flow Matching follows a Wasserstein geodesic, a boundary-value problem. The two reach the same endpoints along different paths.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: relates diffusion models to optimal transport geodesics in Wasserstein space, providing geometric foundation for bridge processes
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 将扩散模型与Wasserstein空间中的最优输运测地线联系起来,为桥过程提供几何基础
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 将扩散模型与Wasserstein空间中的最优输运测地线联系起来,为桥过程提供几何基础
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 将扩散模型与Wasserstein空间中的最优输运测地线联系起来,为桥过程提供几何基础
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Shows that diffusion models correspond to gradient flows of KL divergence in Wasserstein space and denoising steps realize JKO scheme, unifying DDPM, DDIM, and flow matching with optimal transport geometry.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 扩散模型虽被广泛应用,但其几何本质尚不清晰.本文揭示了扩散模型与Wasserstein空间最优输运几何的内在联系:前向过程对应自由能梯度流,去噪步骤实现了JKO格式.这一框架将DDPM,DDIM,NCSN和能量匹配等方法统一起来,为扩散桥和最优输运提供了坚实的理论基础.该工作有助于指导新型桥接方法的设计.
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 扩散模型虽被广泛应用,但其几何本质尚不清晰.本文揭示了扩散模型与Wasserstein空间最优输运几何的内在联系:前向过程对应自由能梯度流,去噪步骤实现了JKO格式.这一框架将DDPM,DDIM,NCSN和能量匹配等方法统一起来,为扩散桥和最优输运提供了坚实的理论基础.该工作有助于指导新型桥接方法的设计.
